article_title: World of Warcraft: Midnight Launches With Player Housing, Revamped Quel'Thalas, and the Largest Single Feature Addition in the MMO's History

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: World of Warcraft: Midnight Launches With Player Housing, Revamped Quel'Thalas, and the Largest Single Feature Addition in the MMO's History
content_preview.summary: Blizzard's eleventh WoW expansion brings player housing, four reimagined zones in Quel'Thalas, and a combat UI overhaul. Reviews call it an evolution, not a revolution, with Metacritic at 82.
content_preview.body_excerpt: World of Warcraft: Midnight, the eleventh expansion for Blizzard's two-decade-old MMORPG and the second chapter of its Worldsoul Saga trilogy, launched worldwide on March 2 at 3:00 PM Pacific Time. The expansion centers on a Void invasion of the blood elf homeland of Quel'Thalas, led by the antagonist Xal'atath, and introduces what Blizzard has called the single largest feature addition in the game's history: [player housing](https://news.xbox.com/en-us/2026/03/02/coming-home-world-of-warcraft-n...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Detailed and well-structured gaming article covering all major aspects of the expansion: zones, housing, combat, and reception.
editor_notes.source_verification: Xbox News confirms housing details (faction zones, biomes, The Common, mementos, community features) precisely. PC Gamer confirms premium currency controversy with Hearthsteel. GameSpot returned 403 but article exists. Key concern: Metacritic score (82), CGMagazine (9/10), GamesRadar (4/5) reviews, combat UI overhaul details, and player count figures (214k concurrent, 11M monthly) are NOT sourced from any of the three cited URLs. These claims appear unsupported by the cited sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Housing and zone details verified. However, several claims in the Critical Reception section lack source attribution within the cited URLs: review scores, player numbers, WeakAuras/addon removal claims attributed to 'PC Gamer' but not found in the cited PC Gamer article (which only covers the housing currency issue).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and informative. Good balance of positive features and controversy.
editor_notes.originality: No existing WoW: Midnight article in the archive.
concerns: Multiple unsourced claims in Critical Reception section: Metacritic 82, CGMagazine 9/10, GamesRadar 4/5, player count 214k/11M, and WeakAuras/addon removal claims attributed to PC Gamer but not in the cited article
recommendations: Add direct source URLs for review scores and player count claims, or remove them
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Good gaming coverage with solid housing details. The Critical Reception section contains multiple unsourced claims, but the core article on the expansion itself is well-attributed. Approved with noted concerns about unsourced statistics.
